tools: one {@item gaming set|phb} of your choice.

default: (a) {@item scale mail|PHB} or (b) a {@item leather armor|PHB}
(a) {@filter simple weapon|items|source=phb|category=basic|type=simple weapon} or (b) {@item longsword|phb} or (c) a {@item rapier|PHB}
(a) {@item light crossbow|PHB} and {@item Crossbow Bolts (20)|PHB|20 bolts} or (b) five {@item javelin|phb|javelins}
(a) a {@item scholar's pack|PHB} or (b) an {@item explorer's pack|PHB}

entries.name: The Warlord
entries: As the aging man struggled to keep up with his companions, he started to regret his decision to join the young adventurers. His days of glory were passed, but hearing the poorly devised plan of the aspiring heroes rekindled something inside of him that he thought was gone forever. He could not use a sword like he used to, but if his young companions took his advice, they may yet be able to survive the dangerous life adventure for a few days.

The dwarven lord ordered his soldiers to form ranks as the unending horde of horrors poured forth from the tunnel ahead. He positioned his elite warriors in the last tunnel before the door to his clan's great city. His clan had delved too greedily and they had freed {@i something} from the depths below. He muttered a final prayer and prepared to lead his men to their deaths, hopefully giving the rest of his clan enough time to escape the horde of monstrosities.

A lithe wood elf quietly led her hunting party through the underbrush of the great wood. They had tracked their foe, a great two-headed troll, for almost two days. As it rested in a clearing, she gave the signal, and her fellow hunters fanned out around the beast, blending in with the natural landscape. On her mark, the hunting party lunged forward with their spears, and the troll was felled before it could react.
All of these warriors are considered Warlords, martial leaders who use their knowledge of tactics and warfare to empower their allies and thwart the plans of their foes.

entries.name: Decisive Leaders
entries: While Warlords are as varied as styles of leadership, they all share one thing in common: a commitment to their decisions. Once a true Warlord decides on a course of action, whether it was their idea or not, they will see the plan through to its end.

These natural-born leaders stand as stalwart figures in the face of danger and can rally their allies to overcome whatever obstacles may stand between them and their goals. Though they are not always formally appointed leaders, the presence of a Warlord, both on and off the field of battle, can motivate those follow them to be the best version of themselves.

entries.name: From Many, One
entries: Alone, a Warlord is a minor threat, but with loyal companions to organize and command, the greatest Warlords are capable of toppling empires. Known for their keen use of tactics and knowledge of warfare, a Warlord is able to draw out the true potential of their allies. Leaders to their core, a Warlord will make sure to understand the capabilities and weaknesses of their allies so they can help them to reach their true potential.

These exceptional strategists are able to step back and allow their allies to excel in combat. Though they are able to hold their own in battle, a warlord's true strength lies in the potent orders they issue to their allies. These insights push their comrades from mundane warriors to true heroes.

entries.name: Creating Your Warlord
entries: When creating your Warlord, consider where they learned to be a leader. Did they serve as the squire of a world-renowned general? Were they the star pupil of their kingdom's military academy? Or, did they slowly rise through the bloody ranks of a band of mercenaries, learning to lead through necessity?

Also, consider why your Warlord would lead others rather than improve their own individual martial skill. Do they see value in teamwork and coordination? Does their age or some other impairment prevent them from fighting on their own? Have they always had a sharp mind and a knack for leading others, maybe even from a young age?

entries.name: Multiclassing and the Warlord
entries: If your group uses the optional multiclassing rule, here's what you need to know when you choose to take your first level in the Warlord class.

entries.name: Ability Score Minimum
entries: As a multiclass character, you must have a minimum of 13 in either Strength or Dexterity, and in either Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma to take a Warlord level, or to take a level in another class if you're already a Warlord.

entries.name: Proficiencies
entries: If Warlord is not your initial class, here are the proficiencies you gain when you take your first Warlord level: light armor, medium armor, shields, simple weapons, and one gaming set.

entries.name: Exploits
entries: If you learn Exploits from more than one source, use the rules {@link Linked Here|https://www.gmbinder.com/share/-NGUL51kfZCPlESxL1wq} to determine your total number of Exploit Dice and Exploits Known.

entries.name: Quick Build
entries: You can make a Warlord quickly by using these suggestions. First, make either Strength or Dexterity your highest ability score, followed by one of Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma. Second, choose the {@background Noble} background.

classFeature.name: Captain
entries: You lead with personality, bravery, and boldness. Charisma is your Leadership Score. You also gain proficiency in heavy armor and your choice of either {@skill Intimidation} or {@skill Persuasion}.

classFeature.name: Inspiring Word
entries: Your words encourage others to fight on. At 1st level, you can use a bonus action to shout at another creature within 30 feet that can hear you. Your target regains hit points equal to one roll of its Hit Die + your Leadership modifier. Though it does not expend a Hit Die as part of this ability.
You can use this feature the number of times indicated in the Inspiring Word column on the Warlord table. You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Leadership Style
entries: At 1st level, you choose your Leadership Style. It determines your Leadership Ability Score and grants additional benefits:

classFeature.name: Mentor
entries: You lead through wise counsel. Wisdom is your Leadership Score. When another creature within 15 feet that can hear you misses an attack or fails an ability check, you can use your reaction to grant it a {@dice 1d4} bonus to its roll.

classFeature.name: Strategist
entries: You lead with cunning tactical insight. Intelligence is your Leadership Score. After you roll initiative, you can switch places in initiative order with another willing creature.

classFeature.name: Exploit Dice
entries: The Warlord table shows the number of Exploit Dice you have to perform Exploits you know. Most of your Exploits will require you to spend Exploit Dice to use them. You can only use one Exploit per attack, ability check, or saving throw, and you regain all expended Exploit Dice when you finish a short or long rest.

Your Exploit Dice begin as {@dice d4}s and increase in size as you gain levels in this class, as shown in the Exploit Die column of the Warlord table. Also, whenever you add an Exploit Die to a roll, always roll it and add it to the result.

classFeature.name: Fighting Style
entries: At 2nd level, you learn one {@filter Fighting Style|optionalfeatures|feature type=fs:wd} from the list at the end of this class. You cannot learn the same Fighting Style more than once, even if a feature allows you to learn another. To learn a Fighting Style, you must meet its prerequisites.
When you gain a level, you can replace this Fighting Style with another Fighting Style available to the Warlord class.

classFeature.name: Tactical Exploits
entries: Also at 2nd level, you learn techniques which enhance your tactical skills. You gain the following features:

entries.name: Exploits Known
entries: You know two {@filter Tactical Exploits|optionalfeatures|feature type=LL:TE} of your choice from the list at the end of this class. To learn a Tactical Exploit, you must meet any prerequisites that it has.

You learn additional Exploits at certain levels, as shown in the Exploits Known column of the Warlord table. Finally, whenever you gain a level, you can replace one Exploit you know with another Tactical Exploit of your choice.

entries.name: Saving Throws
entries: If one of your Tactical Exploits requires a creature to make a saving throw, your Exploit save DC is calculated as follows:

classFeature.name: Tactical Skill
entries: Dedicated training has also honed your skills. Whenever you make an ability check with a tool or skill you are proficient in, you can expend an Exploit Die and add it to your roll. You can do so after you roll the d20 but before you know the outcome.

classFeature.name: Academy of War
entries: At 3rd level, choose the Academy of War from the list here that best represents the training and tactics of your Warlord:
Your Academy grants you features at 3rd level, and again at 6th, 14th, and 18th level in this class. If an Academy feature forces a saving throw it uses your Exploit save DC.

entries.name: Academy Exploits
entries: Some Academies have a list of Tactical Exploits you learn at the levels noted in their description. The Exploits don't count against your Exploits Known and can't be replaced. If you do not meet their prerequisites, you learn them regardless.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn. Moreover, if you use your action to {@action Dash} or to {@action Disengage} you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action on that turn.

classFeature.name: Academy Feature
entries: At 6th level, you gain a feature granted by your Academy of War.

classFeature.name: Captain
entries: You can target creatures that are {@condition Unconscious}, but not dead, with your Inspiring Word, even though they cannot hear you.

classFeature.name: Mentor
entries: The range of your Mentor reaction increases to 30 feet, and when you use this reaction, you can expend an Exploit Die to use it in place of the normal d4 you would roll.

classFeature.name: Strategist
entries: You and creatures of your choice within 15 feet of you gain a bonus to Initiative rolls equal to your Intelligence modifier, so long as you are not {@status surprised} or {@condition Incapacitated}.

classFeature.name: Valiant Leader
entries: Your skills have been honed in the crucible of war. Starting at 7th level, your Leadership Style grants you further benefits:

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Rallying Cry
entries: You rally allies to find success when they might otherwise fail. Starting at 9th level, when another creature that can hear you within 30 feet fails a saving throw, you can use your reaction to rally it. The creature rolls its saving throw again, and adds your Leadership modifier to its new result.
You can use this reaction once per short or long rest. You gain second use at 13th level and a third use at 17th level.

classFeature.name: Unwavering Will
entries: You are not given the luxury of succumbing to base instincts. Beginning at 10th level, you have advantage on saving throws to resist being {@condition Charmed}, {@condition Frightened}, or {@condition Stunned}.

classFeature.name: Tactical Superiority
entries: You are a leader of legendary status. Starting 11th level, you regain one expended use of Inspiring Word and Rallying Cry when you roll initiative. Moreover, the range of your Tactical Exploits, Inspiring Word, and Rallying Cry is doubled.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Academy Feature
entries: At 14th level, you gain a feature granted by your Academy of War.

classFeature.name: Captain
entries: Creatures that you target with Inspiring Word, Rallying Cry, or a Tactical Exploit gain temporary hit points equal to your Charisma modifier. However, these only last for 1 minute.

classFeature.name: Exalted Leader
entries: You are an unflinching leader, even in dire circumstances. At 15th level, your Leadership Style grants you these benefits:

classFeature.name: Mentor
entries: Your Inspiring Word can end one of the following conditions in place of restoring the target's hit points: {@condition Blinded}, {@condition Charmed}, {@condition Deafened}, {@condition Frightened}, {@condition Poisoned}, or {@condition Stunned}.

classFeature.name: Strategist
entries: When you use Inspiring Word, the target can choose to move up to its speed without provoking {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Academy Feature
entries: At 18th level, you gain a feature granted by your Academy of War.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Dauntless
entries: You rival the great commanders and conquerors of legend. At 20th level, you can use Rallying Cry an unlimited number of times. Moreover, whenever you use Inspiring Word you treat the hit points restored as the maximum possible result.

activation.condition: When another creature within range that can hear you misses an attack or fails an ability check
When another creature within 30 feet that can hear you misses an attack or fails an ability check
When another creature that can hear you within 30 feet fails a saving throw

subclassFeature.name: Academy of Chivalry
entries: Warlords that choose the Academy of Chivalry often hail from the ranks of nobility and are almost always formal knights. As leaders, they hold themselves and those who follow them to a lofty code of conduct. These knights stand as beacons of fair play, honor, and justice, both on and off the field of battle.

subclassFeature.name: Chivalric Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Warlord levels noted in the table below. They do not count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level:

subclassFeature.name: Inspiring Shout
entries: Your presence on the field of battle inspires those who fight beside you. When you roll initiative you can expend a use of Inspiring Word and choose a number of creatures within 30 feet that can hear you equal to your Leadership modifier to gain temporary hit points equal to your Exploit Die + your Leadership modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Knighthood
entries: You have been trained in the classical skills of knighthood. You gain proficiency with all martial weapons, one musical instrument, and either {@skill History}, {@skill Performance}, or {@skill Religion}.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Lead the Charge
entries: You lead best from the front lines. When you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn and make at least one melee attack, you can issue an {@optfeature attack order|LLExploits} as a bonus action on that turn.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Flames of Hope
entries: Once between each short or long rest you can use your action to let forth a heroic cry that inflames the hearts of your allies and inspires terror in your foes. Creatures that can hear you within 30 feet gain the corresponding effect below:

entries.name: Allies
entries: Friendly creatures gain temporary hit points equal to your Exploit Die + your Leadership modifier. While these last, the creature cannot be {@condition Charmed} or {@condition Frightened}.

entries.name: Enemies
entries: Hostile creatures must make a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition Frightened} of you. They repeat the saving throw at the end of each of their turns and when they take damage, ending the effect on a success.

subclassFeature.name: Paragon of Chivalry
entries: You stand as an exemplar of chivalric virtue, a shining beacon for those who follow you. You are immune to the {@condition Frightened} and {@condition Charmed} conditions, and creatures of your choice within 30 feet that can see you have advantage on saving throws to resist or end the Charmed and Frightened condition.

subclassFeature.name: Academy of Dread
entries: Where most Warlords have a sense of appreciation and care for those that follow them, those known as Dreadlords only care for one thing - victory. These ruthless captains make use of any possible advantage, often using their enemy's greatest fears against them to turn the tide of the battle. These dark captains are often found leading hordes of orcs and undead.

subclassFeature.name: Dark Captain
entries: You exude an aura of intimidation that shakes all but the most stalwart warriors to their core. You gain proficiency with all martial weapons and in {@skill Intimidation}.
Moreover, whenever you make an Indimidation check, you can use your Leadership ability instead of Charisma. When you do, treat a roll of 7 or lower on the d20 as an 8.

subclassFeature.name: Dread Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Warlord levels in the table below. They don't count against your total Exploits Known and cannot be switched when you gain a level:

subclassFeature.name: Dread Presence
entries: Your presence on the field of battle inspires fear in the hearts of your enemies. As a bonus action, you can expend a use of Inspiring Word to force one creature that can see you within 30 feet to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it is {@condition Frightened} of you for 1 minute. If this Frightened creature sees you take damage, the effect ends.
If the creature flees and is no longer in your line of sight, it can use its action to automatically end this effect.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Merciless
entries: You infuse your commands with your dread willpower. You can use {@optfeature intimidating command|LLExploits} and {@optfeature menacing shout|LLExploits} without expending an Exploit Die a number of times equal to your Leadership modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Also, once per turn when you cause a creature to be {@condition Frightened}, you can instantly issue a single {@optfeature attack order|LLExploits}.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Ruthless Command
entries: You spread fear and desolation on the battlefield. Creatures that you target with an Inspiring Word or a Tactical Exploit have advantage on all attack rolls against creatures that are {@condition Frightened} until the end of their next turn.

Moreover, when you use your Dread Presence, the creature has disadvantage on its initial Wisdom saving throw if you or a creature under the effects of one of your Exploits has hit it with an attack since the end of your last turn.

subclassFeature.name: Baleful Presence
entries: You are a walking avatar of fear. When you force a creature within 30 feet of you to make a saving throw to resist being {@condition Frightened}, it has disadvantage on its roll.
Moreover, creatures within 30 feet that are Frightened of you have their speed reduced to 0 while they can see you.

subclassFeature.name: Dreadlord
entries: You stand as a sinister monument to all those who have tried, and failed, to stop your iron conquest. You are immune to the {@condition Frightened} condition, and you add your Leadership modifier (a minimum of +1) to any attack rolls that you make against creatures that are Frightened of you.

subclassFeature.name: Academy of Ferocity
entries: Warlords who hail from this Academy rarely study in formal schools of war. In place of military theory, their strategies are based on the instincts of predatory hunters. Famous for their ruthless treatment of foes, these leaders would lay down their lives for any member of their pack without hesitation.

subclassFeature.name: Ferocious Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Warlord levels noted in the table below. They do not count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level:

subclassFeature.name: Packleader
entries: You lead your pack in the hunt. As a bonus action, you can mark a creature you can see within 30 feet as your Prey. It remains your Prey for 1 minute, but the effect ends early if your Prey is reduced to 0 hit points, you are {@condition Incapacitated}, or you mark another creature as your Prey.
Any creature you target with Inspiring Word or a Tactical Exploit adds your Exploit Die to the first attack roll it makes against your Prey before the start of your next turn.

You can mark a creature as your Prey a number of times equal to your Leadership modifier (minimum of once). You regain one expended use when you finish a short rest, and all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Predatory Instinct
entries: You hunt as a wild predator. You gain proficiency with martial weapons and in either {@skill Perception} or {@skill Survival}.
In addition, whenever you make a Perception or Survival check based on your sense of smell or hearing, you can treat a roll of 7 or lower on the d20 as an 8.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Silent Stalker
entries: You direct your pack to hunt silently. You and creatures of your choice within 30 feet of you have advantage on {@skill Stealth} checks and can travel stealthily moving at a normal pace.
Also, while you have a creature marked as your Prey, you can issue a {@optfeature maneuvering order|LLExploits} as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Thrill of the Hunt
entries: A successful hunt stokes your blood lust. When your Prey is killed, you can use your reaction to mark another creature in range, then you gain one of the following benefits:

items: You gain temporary hit points equal to your Warlord level.
You regain one expended use of Inspiring Word.
You regain one expended Exploit Die.

subclassFeature.name: Apex Predator
entries: Your primal ferocity invigorates your packmates. Your speed increases by 10 feet, and your weapon attacks against your Prey deal bonus damage equal to your Exploit Die.
Moreover, any creature you target with an Inspiring Word, Rallying Cry, or one of your Tactical Exploits also gains the benefits above until the beginning of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Academy of Gallantry
entries: Sometimes called Skalds, Warlords of Gallantry combine the arts of music, strategy, and magic to inspire allies and vex their foes. Learning a unique style of bardic magic that augments their leadership skills, Gallantry Warlords can turn even an unskilled rabble into a deadly fighting force.

subclassFeature.name: Gallant Spellcasting
entries: Your talent in poetry and music allows you to produce spells, much like the Bard does. You gain the following benefits:

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Gallant Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your spells of 1st-level and higher. To cast a spell, you must expend a slot of that spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest. For example, if you know the {@spell thunderwave} spell and have both a 1st and a 2nd-level spell slot, you can cast thunderwave using either spell slot.

entries.name: Spells Known of 1st-Level and Higher
entries: You know three 1st-level spells of your choice from the {@filter Gallantry spell list|spells|subclass=Warlord: Gallantry (LLAW)} on the following page. The Spells Known column of the Gallant Spellcasting table shows when you learn more spells from this list of 1st-level and up. Any spells you learn must be of a level for which you have spell slots as shown on the table.
When you gain a level, you can replace one of your Spells Known with another spell from the Galantry spell list. The spell must also be of a level for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use any musical instrument you are proficient with as a spellcasting focus for your spells. Gallantry Warlords are known for one-handed instruments, like war horns, while holding a weapon in their other hand.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Use your Leadership modifier when setting the saving throw DC or making a spell attack roll for any Gallantry spells you know through this feature:

subclassFeature.name: Warrior Poet
entries: You combine martial skill and musical talent to inspire your allies. You gain proficiency with two musical instruments of your choice, and in {@skill Performance}.
Moreover, whenever you make a Performance check that includes both a weapon and a musical instrument, you gain a bonus to your roll equal to your Warlord level.

subclassFeature.name: Gallant Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Warlord levels noted in the table below. They do not count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level:

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Heroic Charge
entries: When you roll initiative, you can expend a use of Inspiring Word to rally a heroic charge. You, and any creatures of your choice within 15 feet that can hear you gain the benefits of the {@action Dash} action during your first turn in combat. You cannot use this feature if you are {@status surprised} or {@condition Incapacitated}.
At 11th level, the range of this feature increases to 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Songs of War & Peace
entries: You conduct battle like a great symphony. When you expend a spell slot or Exploit Die as part of your action, you can make a single weapon attack as a bonus action on that turn.
In addition, any creature that spends a short rest with you has advantage on Hit Die rolls to regain its hit points.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Warsong
entries: The Skalds of legend were said to be able to turn the tide of the most desperate battles. Whenever you target a creature with Inspiring Word or a Tactical Exploit, it has advantage on the first ability check, attack roll, or saving throw it makes before the beginning of your next turn.
It also can't be {@condition Charmed} or {@condition Frightened} for that duration.

subclassFeature.name: Mythic Voice
entries: Your voice itself has been infused with the powerful magic you channel. Whenever you cast a 1st-level Gallantry spell, you can choose to expend a use of Inspiring Word instead of a spell slot. You must use a musical instrument as the spellcasting focus for the spell.

subclassFeature.name: Academy of Schemes
entries: Warlords of the Academy of Schemes will secure their victory at any cost. The ultimate utilitarians, they care not how they achieve their goals. They will cheat, deceive, and sacrifice whatever is necessary to succeed. These Warlords always have an escape plan if things do not go as planned.

entries.name: Scheming Adventurers
entries: When playing a character with questionable morals and motivations, like a Warlord of the Academy of Schemes, it is important to remember that D&D is a team game where cooperation is expected.

Masters of schemes leverage any advantages at their disposal, including the abilities of their allies. Make sure to include the other players in any plans your Schemes Warlord may have. Remember that a Warlord is nothing without allies to command!

subclassFeature.name: Cheap Shot
entries: You aren't above dishonorable tricks to secure victory. Once per turn when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can force it to make a Dexterity saving throw, along with the damage.
On a failure, it cannot take reactions and its speed is halved until the start of your next turn. On its next turn, it can only take one action or bonus action, not both.

subclassFeature.name: Dastardly Talents
entries: You will use any method necessary to achieve your aims. You gain proficiency in {@skill Deception}, with {@item Disguise Kit|PHB|Disguise Kits}, and {@item Poisoner's Kit|PHB|Poisoner's Kits}. You can also use your Leadership ability, in place of Charisma, when making Deception checks.

subclassFeature.name: Scheming Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Warlord levels in the table below. They do not count against your number of Exploits Known and they cannot be switched upon gaining a level:

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Ruthless Focus
entries: You direct your allies to prey upon the weak. When a creature fails its saving throw against your Cheap Shot, you can instantly issue an {@optfeature insightful order|LLExploits}, but the creature only gains the benefits of your insightful order if it attacks the target of your Cheap Shot.
Also, if you use a Tactical Exploit as part of your action, you can take the {@action Disengage} or {@action Hide} action as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Devious Tactics
entries: You have no qualms about leaving others in danger. When a creature you can see targets you with an attack or forces you to make a saving throw, you can use your reaction to force a creature within 5 feet of you (other than the attacker) to make a Dexterity saving throw.
On a failure, you switch places, and it becomes the target of that attack or saving throw. The creature can choose to fail.

subclassFeature.name: Inscrutable Mind
entries: Your devious mind is impossible to penetrate. Your thoughts and dreams cannot be read by magic unless you allow it. If a creature attempts to read your mind you can deceive it with false thoughts by making a {@skill Deception} check.

subclassFeature.name: Marked for Death
entries: You relentlessly pursue the destruction of your foes. When a creature fails its saving throw against Cheap Shot, you can choose to mark the creature for death. The first attack to hit that creature before the beginning of your next turn becomes an automatic critical hit.
Once you mark a creature for death you must complete a short or long rest before you can use this feature again.

subclassFeature.name: Advanced Tactics
entries: Your ability to learn and execute strategies in combat exceeds most other commanders and even formally trained Warlords. You gain the following benefits to your Warlord features:

entries.name: Exploits
entries: You learn two additional 1st-degree {@filter Tactical Exploits|optionalfeatures|feature type=LL:TE} of your choice. These Exploits do not count against your number of Exploits Known, but they can be replaced.
You learn additional Tactical Exploits following the same rules at certain levels: at 5th level you learn two 2nd-degree Exploits, and at 9th level you learn one 3rd-degree Exploit.

entries.name: Exploit Dice
entries: Your total number of Exploit Dice increases by 1, and all of your Exploit Dice increase to become {@dice d6}s.
At certain Warlord levels your Exploit Dice increase again; at 5th level ({@dice d8}), at 11th level ({@dice d10}), and at 17th level ({@dice d12}).

subclassFeature.name: Academy of Tactics
entries: Warlords of this Academy most often come from the ranks of formal organizations that serve the elite. Tacticians are highly educated commanders and are known for their dedication to those who follow them. Their understanding of how to best utilize each of their allies' strengths and avoid weaknesses makes them especially deadly opponents on the battlefield.

subclassFeature.name: Strategic Adjustments
entries: You are constantly adapting to meet any challenge. During a long rest, you can spend 1 hour reviewing strategies and tactics and replace a Tactical Exploit you know with another of the same Degree.
When you reach 6th level in this class, you can use this feature during a short rest.

subclassFeature.name: The Art of War
entries: You have studied the great histories of warfare and combat. You gain proficiency in {@skill History} and two Gaming Sets of your choice, and you add double your Proficiency Bonus to any ability check that uses these proficiencies.

subclassFeature.name: Brains over Brawn
entries: You wield your comrades as other warriors wield weapons. If you only use Tactical Exploits with your entire action, you can use your bonus action to take the {@action Disengage} or {@action Search} action, or to use one Order Exploit, such as {@optfeature attack order|LLExploits}.

subclassFeature.name: Know Your Enemy
entries: You have a gift for identifying weak points. When you take the {@action Search} action, you can focus on a creature you can see within 30 feet (DC = 8 + its Challenge Rating). If you have seen it get hit by an attack, add your Warlord level to this roll.
On a success, you learn one characteristic of your choice from the list below. On a failure, you can't use this feature on this creature again until you see it get hit by another attack:

rows: One Trait or Action from its stat block

subclassFeature.name: Gifted Strategist
entries: You are always one step ahead of your foes. You cannot be {@status surprised} while conscious, and when you roll initiative, you can use one Tactical Exploit or take the {@action Ready} action.

subclassFeature.name: Master Tactician
entries: You have a plan in place for every eventuality. You learn the {@optfeature contingency plan|LLExploits} Exploit, but it doesn't count against your total number of Exploits Known. At the end of a long rest, you can use contingency plan without expending an Exploit Die to put the plan in place. You can only have a single contingency plan in place at any one time.

subclassFeature.name: Academy of Claws
entries: You put your tactical knowledge and leadership skill to use in taming of exotic monsters for use in battle. Known for their Monstrous Minions, Warlords of the Claw can exert their will to dominate even the most wild creatures. Some even say the most powerful of these Warlords bring mythical monsters to heel, commanding them as they would a normal soldier.

subclassFeature.name: Claw Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Warlord levels noted in the table below. They do not count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level:

subclassFeature.name: Monstrous Minion
entries: You can exert your considerable will to dominate creatures, pressing them into service as a Minion, using these rules:

entries.name: Subjugating a Minion
entries: As an action, you force a creature within 10 feet that can hear you to make a Charisma saving throw. If the creature is Friendly to you, it automatically fails. On a failed save, that creature becomes your Minion.
If a creature succeeds on this saving throw, it is immune to this ability for the next 24 hours and cannot be targeted. You can only have one Monstrous Minion at a time.

entries.name: Potential Minions
entries: The creatures you can dominate are limited only by your willpower. You can only use this feature on a {@loader Beast|creature/LaserLlama; Compendium of Beasts.json} or Monstrosity with a Challenge Rating equal to your Warlord level divided by three, rounded down.

entries.name: Minion Statistics
entries: Your Minion obeys you with total loyalty. It continues to use its normal stat block, and it gains the benefits from short and long rests.

entries.name: Combat
entries: In combat, your Minion acts during your turn. It can move and use its reaction on its own, but will only take the {@action Dodge} action unless you use a bonus action to order it to take one action from its stat block or another action.
If you are {@condition Incapacitated}, your Minion reverts to its wild state, normally fleeing to return to its home in the wilderness.

entries.name: Exploits
entries: You can target your Minion with Exploits, and it can understand you even if it can't speak.

entries.name: Duration
entries: A creature remains a Minion until it is slain. It returns to its wild state sooner if it can't see or hear you for 1 hour, or if you attempt to dominate another creature and turn it into your Minion.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Iron Command
entries: Whenever your Minion makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw within 15 feet of you, you can use your reaction to add your Leadership modifier to the result of its roll.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Monstrous Menagerie
entries: Your impressive will allows you to tame fearsome monsters. You can target Aberrations, Dragons, Giants, Oozes, Plants, and Undead with Monstrous Minion, so long as their CR is within the normal Challenge Rating guidelines.
Also, once a creature has been your Minion for at least 24 hours, it remains Friendly to you indefinitely unless you give it a reason to become hostile toward you again.

subclassFeature.name: Total Domination
entries: Your unflinching willpower allows you to totally dominate the creatures under your control. The range of Iron Command is 30 feet, and while a Minion is within that range, it is resistant to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

subclassFeature.name: Academy of Counsel
entries: You have learned much from the battlefield, and it is time to pass that knowledge on to the next generation of heroes. As a member of the Academy of Counsel, you have dedicated your life to the education of your Protege, a successor who you will train to take your place on the field of battle.

subclassFeature.name: Counselor Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Warlord levels noted in the table below. They don't count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level.

subclassFeature.name: Counselor's Protege
entries: You have adopted a student to educate in the ways of warfare, your Protege. You determine their appearance, race, and size (either Small or Medium). Proteges are always inexperienced, and often young, but they are eager to learn from you. Your Protege uses the following rules:

entries.name: Statistics
entries: Your Protege is Friendly to you and to your allies. They are fiercely loyal, following orders to the best of their abilities, and never acting outside of your directions. They use the {@creature Protege|LLAW:E} stat block below, which uses your Proficiency Bonus (PB), Leadership modifier, and Exploit save DC.

entries.name: Weapons & Armor
entries: Your Protege is proficient in light and medium armor, shields, and simple weapons. They are equipped with leather armor, a shield, a shortbow and a dagger, but you can give them other equipment.

entries.name: Combat
entries: In combat, your Protege acts during your turn. They can move and use their reaction on their own, but they will only take the {@action Dodge} action unless you use your bonus action to command them to take an action from their stat block, or another action. If you are {@condition Incapacitated}, your Protege acts on their own, and will defend you to the best of their ability.

entries.name: Dying
entries: If your Protege is reduced to 0 hit points, they make death saving throws like a player character. When they are at 0 hit points, you can use your action to touch them and expend one Exploit Die to instantly stabilize them.

entries.name: Recruiting a New Protege
entries: If your Protege dies, you can spend 8 hours seeking out a worthy Humanoid to serve you as a new Protege, so long as a willing student can be found. This Humanoid then uses the Protege stat block.

entries.name: Your Protege & Other Features
entries: Your Protege is a blank slate that you will use your knowledge to educate. To have a Protege better represent their heritage, talk with your DM about adding racial features, a background, or other similar features to your Protege's stat block.
However, any additional features added to your Protege will increase their power and should be done cautiously.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Invigorating Orders
entries: Your words inspire your Protege to acts of heroism. When you target your Protege with an Exploit, they have advantage on their next ability check, attack roll, or saving throw.
Your Protege's skills have increased. They gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws, heavy armor and martial weapons.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Exalted Protege
entries: Your dedication and patience has paid off. Your Protege has become a hero in their own right and now uses the {@creature Exalted Protege|LLAW:E} stat block on this page.

You also learn the {@optfeature heroic order|LLExploits} Exploit. It does not count against your Exploits Known and it cannot be replaced. Once per long rest, you can use this Exploit targeting your Protege without counting against the once per rest use of the Exploit.

subclassFeature.name: Legendary Tandem
entries: Your knowledge of your Protege and their understanding of you are such that you fight as one mind on the field of battle. While you and your Protege are within 30 feet of each other, you both have advantage on your saving throws, and neither of you can have disadvantage on attack rolls.
Finally, your Protege can use their Heroic Determination Trait once between each short or long rest. At 20th level, replacing an attack roll with this Trait makes it an automatic critical hit.

subclassFeature.name: Academy of Liberty
entries: Often forced into positions of leadership, Warlords of Liberty come from the ranks of the common folk. When a dark tyrant begins to oppress the weak, when a monarch raises taxes, or when a village is conquered by invaders, leaders will arise to lead their peoples' resistance against injustice.

subclassFeature.name: Coordinated Assault
entries: While individually you may not be a threat, together you and your comrades can triumph over any enemy. You can issue a {@optfeature support order|LLExploits} as a bonus action on each of your turns.

subclassFeature.name: Liberty Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Warlord levels noted in the table below. They do not count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level:

subclassFeature.name: Salt of the Earth
entries: You hail from the ranks of the common folk, and a life of hard work has made you tough. Your hit point maximum increases by 3, and increases by 1 again whenever you gain a level.
Also, whenever you make an ability check to interact with common people, such as farmers, laborers, or foot soldiers, you gain a bonus to your roll equal to your Exploit Die.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Band Together
entries: Separated you are weak, but together you are strong! When a creature within 15 feet of you takes damage, you can use your reaction to rally allies to their side. You divide the damage the creature would take evenly among willing creatures within 15 feet of it. All creatures must be able to hear you to participate.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Strength in Numbers
entries: The more comrades you rally to your cause, the greater your leadership potential. When you roll for initiative, you regain a number of your expended Exploit Dice equal to the number of allied creatures within 15 feet of you.

You also learn the {@optfeature equip militia|LLExploits} Exploit. It does not count against your Exploits Known, and it cannot be replaced. You can use equip milita during a short or long rest, and once per long rest, you can use it without expending an Exploit Die.

subclassFeature.name: Grand Revolutionary
entries: You are destined to upset the status quo wherever you take up the mantle of leadership. The range of Band Together and Strength in Numbers includes creatures within 30 feet.

Finally, whenever you use Inspiring Word or Rallying Cry, you can target up to three creatures within range, instead of one. For Rallying Cry, all creatures must have failed the same saving throw to be targeted by the same Rallying Cry.

subclassFeature.name: Academy of Navigators
entries: You have trained to lead others on the high seas, far from the rules of established society. You use your skills to keep order, motivate your crew to persevere through hardship, and drive them to new discovery. However, your skills are not limited to the deck of a ship and will serve you well on any battlefield.

subclassFeature.name: Navigator Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Warlord levels noted in the table below. They do not count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level:

subclassFeature.name: Parley
entries: Without established authority to turn to on the sea you have become a shrewd negotiator. As an action, you can expend a use of Inspiring Word and speak to a creature within 15 feet that can see, hear, and understand you, improving its attitude toward you by one. For example, a Hostile creature becomes Indifferent, and an Indifferent creature becomes Friendly.
Once you use this feature on a creature it is immune to its effects for 24 hours. You cannot use this feature on a creature that you or your allies have attacked within the last 24 hours.

subclassFeature.name: Seafarer
entries: Your experience on the high seas has made you an excellent sailor. You gain proficiency with {@item Navigator's Tools|PHB}, Water Vehicles, and in {@skill Perception}. You use your Leadership Ability for all ability checks with these skills.
You also gain the {@optfeature Mariner|LLStyles} Fighting Style, and it can't be replaced. If you already know this Fighting Style you learn a different Warlord Fighting Style.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Navigator's Crew
entries: You have proven your mettle on both land and sea, convincing others to formally join your Crew. During the course of a long rest, you can form a pact with a number of creatures equal to your Leadership modifier. Creatures must be willing and able to understand you to enter this pact.

These creatures become your Crew until you form a new pact, which instantly ends any previous pacts. While aboard the same seafaring vessel, your Crew adds your Leadership modifier to all ability checks related to your vessel.

subclassFeature.name: Rally the Crew
entries: Your presence brings the best out of your Crew. Whenever a member of your Crew is forced to make a saving throw while within 15 feet of you, they add their Proficiency Bonus to the saving throw if they aren't already proficient.
Moreover, you can use a bonus action on each of your turns to issue an Order, such as {@optfeature maneuvering order|LLExploits}, to a member of your Crew that is within 15 feet of you.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: First Mate
entries: You inspire loyalty in the most dastardly scallywags. When you use {@optfeature recruit mercenary|LLExploits}, you can designate them as your First Mate. When you do, you can regain your Exploit Die as normal, the Mercenary gains the benefits of Seafarer and is part of your Crew without counting against your total Crew. You can only have one First Mate at a time.
In place of designating a Mercenary, you can designate a different member of your Crew as First Mate. They gain the benefits of Seafarer and don't count against your total Crew.

subclassFeature.name: Illustrious Admiral
entries: Your skills rival the great sailors and explorers of legend. The range of Rally the Crew increases to 30 feet.
Finally, once you speak with a creature that can hear and understand you, you can place it under the effects of Parley without expending a use of Inspiring Word to do so.

subclassFeature.name: Academy of Order
entries: Civilization thrives within the bounds of lawful society, and you are a defender of that principle. You employ your talent for leadership in the upholding of law and the enforcement of order. You are a thorn in the side of villians, and a shield for all those who live within the bounds of just laws.

subclassFeature.name: Keeper of Order
entries: You employ your tactical training to uphold law and order in civilized society. You gain proficiency in either {@skill Insight} or {@skill Investigation}, and whenever you make an ability check with either of these skills, you can choose to use your Leadership Ability for the check.

In addition, when you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with a non-lethal melee attack, you can use a bonus action on that turn to restrain them with a {@item net|PHB}, rope, {@item manacles|PHB}, or another similar object. The DC to escape this restraint is equal to your Exploit save DC unless its innate escape DC was already higher.

subclassFeature.name: Law's Shield
entries: It is your duty to uphold the law and defend the weak. When a creature you can see within 15 feet is hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to issue it a {@optfeature defensive order|LLExploits}, imposing disadvantage on the triggering attack, possibly turning a hit into a miss.

You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your Leadership modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. When you have no uses, you can expend an Inspiring Word to use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Order Exploits
entries: You learn certain Exploits at the Warlord levels noted in the table below. They do not count against your total number of Exploits Known and can't be switched upon gaining a level:

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Halt
entries: Your words carry lawful authority. When a creature you can see within 30 feet moves, you can use your reaction to spend a use of Inspiring Word and force it to make a Wisdom saving throw. The target must be able to hear and understand you.
On a failed save, its speed is reduced to 0 until the start of its next turn. Once a creature succeeds on this saving throw, it is immune to this feature for the next 24 hours.

subclassFeature.name: Stalwart Defender
entries: You are the shield that holds back the forces of lawlessness and chaos. Once per short or long rest, you can use {@optfeature hold the line|LLExploits} without expending an Exploit Die.
When using hold the line, its range is 15 feet, and you can issue a {@optfeature defensive order|LLExploits} as a bonus action on each turn.

subclassFeature.name: Archetype Exploits
entries: You learn additional exploits for your archetype.

subclassFeature.name: Bastion of Order
entries: You are an unwavering shield for those who live within the bounds of the law. While you are using {@optfeature hold the line|LLExploits}, you can use your Law's Shield and Halt reactions without expending uses of either feature or Inspiring Word.
Moreover, you can move up to 10 feet on your turn without ending the effects of hold the line.

subclassFeature.name: Unerring Eye
entries: Your keen sight pierces all deceptions. You can automatically detect the presence of Illusion magic and Shapeshifters, but you are not able to see the true forms that they disguise.

subclassFeature.name: High Authority
entries: You are the highest authority of the law, only superseded by monarchs and divine agents. You always exude the effects of {@optfeature hold the line|LLExploits} to creatures of your choice within 15 feet of you, but you can move and act as normal.
Finally, when a creature fails its saving throw against Halt, it is also {@condition Restrained} until the start of its next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Academy of Zeal
entries: You have been chosen to lead the armies of your deity in battle against their unholy foes. You lead by a mix of strategy and faith, inspiring comrades with both word and spell. Invoking your divine right to lead, you must stand as a paragon of your faith.

entries.name: Optional Rule: Divine Domain
entries: Talk to your DM about choosing a Divine Domain from those available to the Cleric for your Warlord. Whenever you learn a Zeal spell, you can choose to learn spells from that list of Domain Spells.

subclassFeature.name: Anointed Magic
entries: When you join this Academy of War, you learn to call on divine power in the form of spells, using the rules below:

entries.name: Cantrip
entries: You learn two Cantrips of your choice from the {@filter Zeal spell list|spells|subclass=warlord: zeal (llaware)} at the end of this Academy of War description, and you learn an additional Zeal Cantrip at 10th level.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Zeal Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have, and the level of those spell slots. All of your spell slots from this feature are the same level. To cast one of the Zeal spells you know of 1st-level and higher, you must expend one of these spell slots. You regain all of your spell slots each time you finish a short or long rest.
For example, at 7th level, you have two 2nd-level spell slots. To cast the 1st-level spell {@spell command}, you must spend one of those slots, and command is cast at 2nd-level.

entries.name: Spells Known of 1st-Level and Higher
entries: You learn two 1st-level spells of your choice from the Zeal spell list. The Spells Known column of the Zeal Spellcasting table here shows when you learn additional Zeal spells of 1st-level or higher. A spell you choose must be of a level no higher than what's shown in the table's Slot Level column for your level.
When you gain a level, you can choose one Zeal spell you know and replace it with another spell from the Zeal spell list, which must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You must use a {@item Holy Symbol|PHB} as the Spellcasting Focus for your Zeal spells. If you emblazon your Holy Symbol upon a {@item shield|PHB}, you can use it as a Spellcasting Focus for your Zeal spells while you are wielding it.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Use your Leadership modifier when setting the saving throw DC or making a spell attack roll for any Zeal spells you know through this feature:

subclassFeature.name: Divine Mandate
entries: You have been divinely appointed to lead others in war. You know the {@optfeature attack order|LLExploits} Exploit. It does not count against your total number of Exploits Known, and it cannot be replaced.
You also gain proficiency in {@skill Religion}. Whenever you make a Religion check, add your Leadership modifier to your roll.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity
entries: As an action, you can hold aloft your Holy Symbol to Channel the power of your deity to produce effects beyond the bounds of spells. When you Channel Divinity, you produce one of two effects: Anointed Disciple or Mantle of Command.

You can Channel Divinity twice. You regain one use when you finish a short rest, and all uses when you complete a long rest. When you have no uses left, you can expend an Anointed Magic spell slot to Channel Divinity again.

subclassFeature.name: Anointed Disciple
entries: As an action, you grasp a Holy Symbol and Channel Divinity to fill yourself with divine presence. For 10 minutes, you add your Exploit Die to all ability checks you make that use your Leadership Ability.

subclassFeature.name: Mantle of Command
entries: As an action, hold aloft your Holy Symbol and fill yourself with divine leadership for 1 minute. For the duration, you can use a bonus action on each turn to make a weapon attack or issue one Order, like {@optfeature attack order|LLExploits}.

subclassFeature.name: Words of Zeal
entries: Your voice has been infused with divine authority. When you use Inspiring Word, the target regains the maximum number of hit points, in place of rolling its Hit Die.

Moreover, when you target a creature with an {@optfeature attack order|LLExploits}, it can choose for its weapon attacks on that following turn to deal radiant damage in place of its normal damage type.

subclassFeature.name: Favored Servant
entries: You have proven your dedication to the divine cause of your deity and been rewarded. When you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn, you can cast a Zeal spell with a casting time of one action in place of one of your attacks.
Finally, whenever you expend an Anointed Magic spell slot, you regain one of the following: hit points equal to your level, one Exploit Die, or one use of Inspiring Word.

target.affects.special: Target of Inspiring Word, Rallying Cry, or one of your Tactical Exploits

activation.condition: Whenever you make a Performance check that includes both a weapon and a musical instrument
Once per turn when you hit a creature with a weapon attack
When a creature fails its saving throw against your Cheap Shot
When a creature you can see targets you with an attack or forces you to make a saving throw
When a creature fails its saving throw against Cheap Shot
Whenever your Minion makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw within range
Whenever you make an ability check to interact with common people
During the course of a long rest
When a creature you can see within 15 feet is hit by an attack
When a creature you can see within 30 feet moves
Whenever you expend an Anointed Magic spell slot
Whenever you expend an Anointed Magic spell slot
Whenever you expend an Anointed Magic spell slot

trait.name: Heroic Determination
entries: Once per long rest, when your Protege misses an attack roll, or fails an ability check or saving throw, they can choose to replace their d20 roll with your Warlord level, possibly making it a success.

trait.name: Hit Dice
entries: Your Protege has a number of {@dice d8} Hit Dice equal to your Warlord level. They also gain the normal benefits of both short and long rests.

trait.name: Quick Student
entries: Each time your PB increases by 1, your Protege's bonus to their saving throws, skills, and their attack bonus also increase by the same amount.

action.name: Extra Attack
entries: When you command your Protege to make a weapon attack, they make two attacks instead of one.

action.name: Greatsword
entries: {@atk mw} +9 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@damage 2d6 +4}) slashing damage.

action.name: Longbow
entries: {@atk rw} +8 to hit, range 150/600, one target. {@h}({@damage 1d8 +3}) piercing damage.

trait.name: Hit Dice
entries: Your Protege has a number of {@dice d8} Hit Dice equal to your Warlord level. They also gain the normal benefits of both short and long rests.

trait.name: Quick Student
entries: Each time your PB increases by 1, your Protege's bonus to their saving throws, skills, and their attack bonus also increase by the same amount.

action.name: Dagger
entries: {@atk mw} +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}({@damage 1d4 +3}) slashing damage.

action.name: Shortbow
entries: {@atk rw} +5 to hit, range 80/320, one target. {@h}({@damage 1d6 +3}) piercing damage.
